channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitics headlines curated from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: 🇨🇳 Chinese scientists land on disputed Sandy Cay reef as Philippines tensions escalate
item.description: Beijing researchers landed on the uninhabited South China Sea sandbank over the weekend in the latest assertion of sovereignty claims, amid ongoing territorial disputes with Manila. (SCMP)

item.title: 🇮🇱 Report reveals Israel operated second military base in Iraqi desert during Iran war
item.description: Haaretz reports Israel ran covert outposts in Iraq during the conflict with Iran, expanding its regional military footprint beyond acknowledged operations. (Haaretz)

item.title: 🇦🇹 Austrian jets intercept US military aircraft two days in a row amid airspace tensions
item.description: Austria scrambled fighter jets to intercept US military planes for the second consecutive day, highlighting growing friction over military flight operations in European airspace. (Defense News)

item.title: ✈️ Israels F-35s to get external fuel tanks after extensive Iran combat operations
item.description: Fresh off combat operations against Iran, Israel is boosting the F-35I Adirs endurance with external fuel tanks to reduce dependence on tanker aircraft for long-range missions. (The War Zone)

item.title: 💰 Middle East oil crisis has already cost global businesses 25 billion dollars
item.description: The economic impact of disrupted Hormuz shipments is mounting rapidly, with Brent crude above 111 and companies worldwide scrambling to secure alternative supply chains. (Oil Price)

item.title: 🦠 WHO Declares Ebola Global Health Emergency in Congo-Uganda Outbreak
item.description: The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern after more than 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths. The CDC confirmed Americans are among those affected. (AP News)

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Earthquake Kills Two, Forces Thousands to Evacuate
item.description: A magnitude 5.2 earthquake in China's southwest Guangxi region killed two people and forced more than 7,000 in the city of Liuzhou to evacuate. Authorities warn of transport disruptions as search and rescue operations continue. (Reuters)

item.title: 📉 Global Bond Rout Deepens as Iran War Fuels Inflation Fears
item.description: Bonds from Tokyo to New York extended losses as rising energy prices from the ongoing Middle East war fanned inflation fears and stoked investor wagers on rate hikes from global central banks. The yield spike poses risks to unprepared equity markets. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇨🇺 Cuba Seeks Russian Drones, Eyes Guantanamo Attack
item.description: US intelligence reports Cuba has purchased 300+ Russian and Iranian drones and is discussing attacks on Guantanamo Bay and Key West, with Iranian military advisors now in Havana. (Kyiv Independent)

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkish Arms Subsidiary Rebrands After Israel Sales Backlash
item.description: Repkon USA, a Turkish-owned defense contractor, rebranded to Paligen Technologies after revelations it would supply bomb bodies for Israel, sparking protests in Istanbul and parliamentary inquiries in Turkey. (Middle East Eye)

item.title: 🇪🇹 Ethiopia-Eritrea War Fears as Tigray Tensions Escalate
item.description: TPLF leader Debretsion Gebremichael reclaimed presidency of Tigray region, challenging the interim administration. Ethiopian fighter jets seen over Mekelle as fears grow of renewed conflict with Eritrea. (Deutsche Welle)

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelensky Touts Shift in Battlefield Balance After Moscow Strike
item.description: Following Ukraines largest drone attack on Moscow in over a year, Zelensky announced a shift in momentum and promised more deep strikes as Russia downed over 3,000 drones in the past week. (Kyiv Independent)

item.title: 🇭🇺 Hungary Opposition Leads Orbán for First Time in 15 Years
item.description: Péter Magyars Tisza party leads Viktor Orbáns Fidesz by 12 points ahead of April elections in what Politico calls the EUs most consequential election of 2026. (Politico)

item.title: 🌊 Climate Change Reducing Oxygen Levels in Rivers Worldwide, Scientists Find
item.description: A new study reveals that global warming is systematically reducing dissolved oxygen in rivers across the globe, creating expanding "dead zones" that threaten aquatic ecosystems and drinking water supplies. (AP News)

item.title: 🇨🇳 China economic growth stalls as output and retail sales miss forecasts
item.description: Industrial output and retail sales sharply undershot expectations in April as the world second-biggest economy wrestled with higher energy costs from the Iran war and persistently weak domestic demand. (Reuters)

item.title: 🛢️ Oil prices climb after drone attack on UAE nuclear plant
item.description: Oil prices extended gains as efforts to end the US-Iran conflict appeared stalled following a drone attack on a UAE nuclear power plant and President Trump prepared to discuss military options on Iran. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇩🇪 German prosecutors expose Russian sanctions-busting network supplying military tech
item.description: A four-year investigation uncovered a Lübeck-based trading firm that routed dual-use components through Turkey into Russia, violating Western sanctions to supply the Kremlin war machine. (Politico)

item.title: 🛢️ Brent crude breaks $111 as Gulf drone attacks and Hormuz fears spark shortage concerns
item.description: Oil prices surged in early Asian trading after drone strikes on UAE and Saudi Arabia, combined with failed Iran diplomacy, raised fears of global supply disruptions. WTI hit $108.20 while Brent climbed to $111.50. (OilPrice)

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 Xi and Trump agree Iran must not obtain nuclear weapons in strategic shift on Gulf security
item.description: China and the US reached a rare point of agreement on Iran policy during Trumps China visit, with both leaders signaling that Tehrans nuclear ambitions must be contained, reshaping Middle East power dynamics. (Gulf News)

item.title: ☢️ UAE Blames Iran for Drone Strike Near Barakah Nuclear Power Plant
item.description: The United Arab Emirates accused Iran or its proxies of launching a drone that sparked a fire just outside the Barakah nuclear facility. No radiation leaks were reported, but the incident marks a dangerous escalation as Iran war ceasefire talks remain stalled. (The Guardian)

item.title: 🇪🇺 NATO Defense Ministers Meet Without US Leadership Amid Questions
item.description: European allies downplayed concerns after US Defense Secretary Hegseth skipped the NATO gathering in Brussels, marking the second consecutive high-level US absence. Germany and Iceland insisted the alliance remains strong despite the unusual US no-shows. (AP News)

item.title: 🇦🇪 Drone Strike Hits UAE Barakah Nuclear Plant Amid Iran War Deadlock
item.description: A drone strike caused a fire at the UAE sole nuclear power plant, with Emirati officials investigating the source and vowing response to what they called a terrorist attack. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇫🇷 G7 Finance Chiefs Convene in Paris to Tackle Trade Imbalances
item.description: Finance ministers from G7 nations meet in Paris to coordinate critical raw material supplies and address global economic tensions amid geopolitical differences threatening group cohesion. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel to Establish Defense Offices in Former UNRWA East Jerusalem Compound
item.description: Israel plans to set up defense offices in the former UNRWA compound in East Jerusalem after demolishing structures, a move the UN agency called a violation of international law. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia Destroyed Over 3,000 Ukrainian Drones in Past Week
item.description: Russian defense ministry reports intercepting and destroying at least 3,124 Ukrainian drones over the past week, as Ukraine continues its drone campaign against Russian targets. (Reuters)
